result:
This confirms: "The reference count of the value object is *not* incremented" — the DB takes ownership of the passed reference without incrementing, so no local `decrRefCount` is needed or present. This validates category (b) sites (926, 983, 1323/1325).

I have all 10 call sites classified. Here's the complete breakdown:

## (a) Locally freed via `decrRefCount` in the same function — 6 sites

| Line | Function | Call | Freed at |
|---|---|---|---|
| 197 | `setGenericCommand` | `createStringObjectFromLongLong(milliseconds)` → `milliseconds_obj` | line 207, after being used as an arg in `rewriteClientCommandArgument`/`rewriteClientCommandVector` |
| 541 | `pexpireCommand`/(expire family) generic handler | `createStringObjectFromLongLong(milliseconds)` → `milliseconds_obj` | line 543, after `rewriteClientCommandVector` |
| 891 | `msetGenericCommand`/`msetCommand` (bulk SET path) | `createStringObjectFromLongLong(milliseconds)` → `milliseconds_obj` | line 894, after `rewriteClientCommandArgument` |
| 1345 | `increxCommand`/`incrDecrGenericCommand` (INCREX path) | `createStringObjectFromLongLong(args.expire_ms)` → `milliseconds_obj` | line 1347, after `rewriteClientCommandVector` |
| 1439 | `lcsCommand` | `createStringObject("",0)` → `obja` (fallback when key `a` missing) | `cleanup:` label, line 1629 `if (obja) decrRefCount(obja);` |
| 1440 | `lcsCommand` | `createStringObject("",0)` → `objb` (fallback when key `b` missing) | `cleanup:` label, line 1630 `if (objb) decrRefCount(objb);` |

In these cases the object is only ever used transiently — as a temporary argument for command rewriting (`rewriteClientCommandArgument`/`rewriteClientCommandVector`, which internally `incrRefCount`s what it keeps) or as a decoded-string stand-in — and is always freed before the function returns.

## (b) Ownership transferred, no local `decrRefCount` — 4 sites

| Line | Function | Call | Transferred to |
|---|---|---|---|
| 926 | `incrDecrCommand` | `createStringObjectFromLongLongForValue(value)` → `new` | `dbReplaceValueWithLink(c->db, c->argv[1], &new, link)` (existing key) or `dbAddByLink(c->db, c->argv[1], &new, &link)` (new key) |
| 983 | `incrbyfloatCommand` | `createStringObjectFromLongDouble(value,1)` → `new` | same as above: `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` |
| 1323 | `increxCommand`/`incrDecrGenericCommand` (float branch) | `createStringObjectFromLongDouble(value_ld,1)` → `new` | `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` |
| 1325 | same function (int branch) | `createStringObjectFromLongLongForValue(value_ll)` → `new` | `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` |

For these four, the object becomes the new database value. `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` both funnel into `dbSetValue`, whose doc comment states explicitly: *"The reference count of the value object is *not* incremented"* — i.e., the DB consumes the caller's reference. Consistent with that, none of these four sites has a matching `decrRefCount` for `new` anywhere in the function; `new` instead lives on as the db-owned object and is passed on to `addReplyLongLongFromStr`/`addReplyBulk`, `keyModified`, and possibly `rewriteClientCommandVector` (read-only uses, no extra ref taken by those on the pointer that got consumed) before the function returns.

**Total: 10 allocation sites — 6 locally freed, 4 ownership-transferred.** (One call site, `createStringObject`, appears twice on adjacent lines 1439/1440, both in category (a).)
